title: QUEST Tutorial
text: tutorial presents complete example analysis using QUEST Stereo-seq spatial transcriptomics data mouse embryo (E9.5). running tutorial, please make sure QUEST package installed. Detailed installation instructions provided Installation page package website. QUEST can applied two complementary ways: step--step workflow, including fuzzy clustering, uncertainty quantification, boundary detection, aggregation detection, visualization; one-step workflow using wrapper function quest(), integrates analysis steps unified pipeline. goal tutorial demonstrate mechanics package, also illustrate uncertainty quantification can provide additional insights beyond conventional hard clustering.

title: QUEST Tutorial
text: first load required package.
code: # devtools::install_github("YanlinTong/QUEST") library(QUEST) library(Matrix)

title: QUEST Tutorial
text: next load example dataset used throughout tutorial, including count matrix spatial coordinates. example data can downloaded following link: Download example data downloading, please place files following directory: data/StereoSeq_Embryo95/
code: load("../data/StereoSeq_Embryo95/dat_combined.RData")

title: QUEST Tutorial
text: count matrix represents gene expression measurements across spatial locations, rows correspond genes columns correspond spatial locations.

title: QUEST Tutorial
text: spatial coordinate matrix specifies physical positions spatial location tissue, row representing one location.

title: QUEST Tutorial
text: QUEST operates low-dimensional embeddings obtained upstream methods. general, use spatially aware principal components (Spatial PCs). following code illustrates Spatial PCs can generated using SpatialPCA. Spatial PCs used tutorial can directly downloaded data link, need recomputed. Spatial PCs matrix following dimensions, rows representing spatial locations columns representing principal components. subset matrix shown illustration.
code: # ST <- CreateSpatialPCAObject( #   counts = dat_cnt, #   location = dat_loc, #   project = "SpatialPCA", #   gene.type = "spatial", #   sparkversion = "sparkx", #   numCores_spark = 5, #   gene.number = 3000, #   min.loctions = 20, #   min.features = 20 # ) #  # ST <- SpatialPCA_buildKernel( #   ST, #   kerneltype = "gaussian", #   bandwidthtype = "SJ", #   bandwidth.set.by.user = NULL # ) #  # ST <- SpatialPCA_EstimateLoading( #   ST, #   fast = FALSE, #   SpatialPCnum = 20 # ) #  # ST <- SpatialPCA_SpatialPCs(ST, fast = FALSE) load("../data/StereoSeq_Embryo95/pc.RData") dim(pc) #> [1] 5912   20 print(pc[1:3, 1:3]) #>               pc1      pc2       pc3 #> 147_104 -3.599563 14.11253 -12.76451 #> 147_105 -4.337513 14.61970 -11.71134 #> 147_106 -4.130162 14.37786 -12.59037

what: Step 1: Fuzzy clustering with automatic fuzziness selection
title: QUEST Tutorial
text: first perform fuzzy c-means clustering automatically select fuzziness parameter mm candidate grid using XB index. provides flexible clustering framework allowing degree fuzziness data-adaptive.

previous_headings: Run QUEST step by step > Step 1: Fuzzy clustering with automatic fuzziness selection
title: QUEST Tutorial
text: important inputs : x: numeric matrix dimension n×dn \times d, rows correspond spatial locations columns correspond features; nclus: number clusters; vec_m: numeric vector candidate fuzziness parameters; nstart: number random initializations; iter_max: maximum number iterations initialization. fitted clustering object can inspected follows. output contains selected fuzziness parameter, membership matrix, hard cluster labels, cluster sizes, additional model information.
code: set.seed(2) obj_fcm <- fcmclust_auto(   x = pc,   nclus = 12,   vec_m = seq(from = 1.1, to = 2.0, by = 0.1),   nstart = 50,   iter_max = 100,   verbose = FALSE ) print(obj_fcm) #> Fuzzy c-means clustering with automatic fuzziness selection #>  #> Selected m: #> [1] 1.3 #>  #> XB index: #> [1] 0.7780135 #>  #> XB index over candidate m (first 5): #>    m           xb #>  1.1    0.8722102 #>  1.2    0.7988497 #>  1.3    0.7780135 #>  1.4    0.9253513 #>  1.5 1036.1610365 #> ... (truncated, use $xb_grid to view all values) #>  #> Corresponding clustering result: #>  #> Fuzzy c-means clustering with 12 clusters and 50 initializations #>  #> Fuzziness parameter (m): #> [1] 1.3 #>  #> Memberships (first 5 observations and first 3 clusters): #>             [,1]         [,2]         [,3] #> [1,] 0.007483206 0.0006129729 0.0004307095 #> [2,] 0.004037587 0.0003647268 0.0002265688 #> [3,] 0.004846589 0.0004151032 0.0002562929 #> [4,] 0.014094660 0.0008083182 0.0005618637 #> [5,] 0.059729192 0.0016189063 0.0012030115 #>  #> Hard cluster labels (first 5 observations): #> [1] "12" "12" "12" "12" "12" #>  #> Cluster sizes: #>  [1] 606 496 425 709 628 554 284 136 470 811 262 531 #>  #> Final objective value: #> 1087835  #>  #> Number of iterations until convergence: #> 14

title: QUEST Tutorial
text: QUEST computes entropy-based uncertainty fuzzy membership matrix, yielding location-level measure clustering ambiguity. Larger entropy values indicate less confident assignments.

previous_headings: Run QUEST step by step > Step 2: Uncertainty quantification
title: QUEST Tutorial
text: important inputs : u: fuzzy membership matrix returned fuzzy clustering. normalized: logical value indicating whether entropy normalized range [0,1][0, 1]. default, entropy normalized, facilitates interpretation comparability across datasets. threshold_method: character string specifying method used define uncertain locations. default "mean_sd", defines uncertainty based mean standard deviation entropy distribution. Alternatively, "quantile" can used define uncertainty based specified quantile. threshold_scale: numeric value controlling threshold threshold_method = "mean_sd". default, uncertain locations defined entropy greater thanmean(entropy)+0.5×sd(entropy). \text{mean}(\text{entropy}) + 0.5 \times \text{sd}(\text{entropy}).  , locations entropy substantially higher threshold classified uncertain.
code: res_uncer <- compute_uncertainty(   u = obj_fcm$membership )

title: QUEST Tutorial
text: next identify boundary uncertain locations based spatial neighborhood structure. uncertain location classified boundary neighboring locations contain multiple distinct cluster labels, including label, include least one certain location.

previous_headings: Run QUEST step by step > Step 3: Boundary detection
title: QUEST Tutorial
text: important inputs : dat_loc: spatial coordinates; cluster: hard cluster labels fuzzy clustering; is_uncertain: logical vector indicating uncertain locations; n_neighbors: number nearest neighbors used boundary detection.
code: res_boundary <- detect_boundary(   dat_loc = dat_loc,   cluster = obj_fcm$cluster,   is_uncertain = res_uncer$is_uncertain,   n_neighbors = 8 )

title: QUEST Tutorial
text: identify aggregated uncertain locations based spatial neighborhood structure. uncertain location classified aggregated substantial proportion neighboring locations also uncertain.

previous_headings: Run QUEST step by step > Step 4: Aggregation detection
title: QUEST Tutorial
text: important inputs : dat_loc: spatial coordinates; is_uncertain: logical vector indicating uncertain locations; n_neighbors: number nearest neighbors used aggregation detection; distance_threshold: distance threshold used construct connectivity graph; min_component_size: minimum connected component size retained aggregated region.
code: res_aggre <- detect_aggregation(   dat_loc = dat_loc,   is_uncertain = res_uncer$is_uncertain,   n_neighbors = 8,   distance_threshold = 1.1,   min_component_size = 20 )

previous_headings: Run QUEST step by step > Step 5: Visualization
title: QUEST Tutorial
text: first visualize inferred cluster labels QUEST examine overall spatial domain structure. point corresponds spatial location colored according assigned cluster.
code: plot_clusters(   dat_loc = dat_loc,   cluster = obj_fcm$cluster,   title = "QUEST clusters",   palette = c(     "#66C2A5", "#FC8D62", "#8DA0CB", "#E78AC3", "#A6D854", "#FFD92F",     "#E5C494", "#B3B3B3", "#1B9E77", "#D95F02", "#7570B3", "#E7298A"   ) ) #> Error in get(paste0(generic, ".", class), envir = get_method_env()) :  #>   object 'type_sum.accel' not found

previous_headings: Run QUEST step by step > Step 5: Visualization
title: QUEST Tutorial
text: next visualize uncertainty spatial location categorical variable. representation facilitates identification highly ambiguous regions provides concise summary spatial uncertainty patterns.
code: plot_uncertainty(   dat_loc = dat_loc,   uncertainty = res_uncer$entropy,   mode = "category",   title = "Uncertainty" )

previous_headings: Run QUEST step by step > Step 5: Visualization
title: QUEST Tutorial
text: visualize boundary uncertain locations. highlighted locations reveal transitional regions spatial domains provide refined characterization domain interfaces hard clustering alone.
code: plot_boundary(   dat_loc = dat_loc,   cluster = obj_fcm$cluster,   is_uncertain = res_uncer$is_uncertain,   is_boundary = res_boundary$is_boundary,   title = "Boundary uncertain locations",   color_mode = "cluster",   palette = c(     "#66C2A5", "#FC8D62", "#8DA0CB", "#E78AC3", "#A6D854", "#FFD92F",     "#E5C494", "#B3B3B3", "#1B9E77", "#D95F02", "#7570B3", "#E7298A"   ) )

previous_headings: Run QUEST step by step > Step 5: Visualization
title: QUEST Tutorial
text: Finally, visualize aggregated uncertain locations. aggregated regions reveal spatially connected clusters uncertainty, highlighting areas structural complexity heterogeneity well captured hard clustering alone.
code: plot_aggregation(   dat_loc = dat_loc,   cluster = obj_fcm$cluster,   is_uncertain = res_uncer$is_uncertain,   is_aggre = res_aggre$is_aggre,   igraph_cluster = res_aggre$aggre_cluster,   title = "Aggregated uncertain locations",   color_mode = "igraph" )

title: QUEST Tutorial
text: convenience, entire workflow can also performed using wrapper function quest(). function integrates clustering, uncertainty quantification, optional boundary aggregation detection, optional plotting. useful compact end--end analysis desired.

title: QUEST Tutorial
text: important inputs : x: low-dimensional embedding matrix; dat_loc: spatial coordinates; nclus: number clusters; vec_m: candidate fuzziness parameters automatic selection used; boundary_n_neighbors: number nearest neighbors used boundary detection; aggregation_n_neighbors: number nearest neighbors used aggregation detection; aggregation_distance_threshold: graph distance threshold aggregation detection; aggregation_min_component_size: minimum connected component size aggregation detection; make_plots: whether generate default plots.
code: set.seed(2) res_quest <- quest(   x = pc,   dat_loc = dat_loc,   nclus = 12,   vec_m = seq(from = 1.1, to = 2.0, by = 0.1),       nstart = 50,   boundary_n_neighbors = 8,   aggregation_n_neighbors = 8,   aggregation_distance_threshold = 1.1,   aggregation_min_component_size = 20,   make_plots = TRUE )

title: QUEST Tutorial
text: output quest() contains clustering results, uncertainty estimates, optional boundary aggregation results, set default visualizations.
code: names(res_quest) #> [1] "clustering"  "uncertainty" "boundary"    "aggregation" "plots"       #> [6] "settings"    "call"

title: QUEST Tutorial
text: plots generated quest(make_plots = TRUE) intended quick visualization. publication-quality figures, users encouraged call plotting functions directly customize graphical parameters. plot shows inferred spatial domains obtained QUEST.  plot visualizes uncertainty spatial location continuous measure.  plot presents uncertainty categorical form.  plot highlights boundary uncertain locations identified QUEST.  plot shows aggregated uncertain regions, clusters uncertain locations form spatially coherent patterns.
code: res_quest$plots$clusters res_quest$plots$uncertainty_continuous res_quest$plots$uncertainty_category res_quest$plots$boundary res_quest$plots$aggregation

title: QUEST Tutorial
text: tutorial, demonstrated QUEST can applied spatial transcriptomics data obtain fuzzy domain assignments, location-level uncertainty estimates, boundary uncertain locations, aggregated uncertain regions. Together, outputs provide richer characterization spatial domain structure conventional hard clustering alone.

title: QUEST
text: QUEST computational method uncertainty-aware spatial domain detection spatial transcriptomics. provides probabilistic domain assignments location-level uncertainty estimates, enabling identification ambiguous regions domain boundaries heterogeneous subdomains. method implemented open-source R package efficient C++ acceleration via Rcpp.

title: QUEST
text: can install latest development version QUEST GitHub:
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("YanlinTong/QUEST")

title: QUEST
text: section demonstrates working example QUEST pipeline. 👉 See full tutorial .

what: QUEST: Quantifying Uncertainty for Spatial Domain Detection in Spatial Transcriptomics — QUEST-package
title: QUEST: Quantifying Uncertainty for Spatial Domain Detection in Spatial Transcriptomics — QUEST-package
text: QUEST computational method quantifying uncertainty spatial domain detection spatial transcriptomics. leveraging fuzzy clustering, QUEST generates probabilistic domain assignments location-level uncertainty estimates, enabling separation confidently assigned locations ambiguous ones. Building uncertainty quantification framework, QUEST facilitates identification novel tissue structures, including transitional boundaries heterogeneous subdomains, improves accuracy downstream analyses prioritizing high-confidence locations, helps guide determination number spatial domains.

title: QUEST: Quantifying Uncertainty for Spatial Domain Detection in Spatial Transcriptomics — QUEST-package
text: Maintainer: Yanlin Tong zoetong@umich.edu Authors: Xiang Zhou contributors: Xiaoquan Wen [contributor] Jiandie Lin [contributor]

title: Calculate the Kwon index — compute_kwon
text: Compute Kwon cluster validity index data, membership matrix, cluster centers.

title: Calculate the Kwon index — compute_kwon
code: compute_kwon(x, u, v, m = 2)

title: Calculate the Kwon index — compute_kwon
text: x numeric matrix dimension \(n \times d\), \(n\) number observations \(d\) number features. u numeric matrix dimension \(n \times k\), \(n\) number observations \(k\) number clusters. row represent membership vector. v numeric matrix dimension \(k \times d\), \(k\) number clusters \(d\) number features. row represent cluster center. m numeric value greater 1 specifying fuzziness parameter.

title: Calculate the Kwon index — compute_kwon
text: numeric value representing Kwon index.

what: Compute uncertainty from membership matrix — compute_uncertainty
title: Compute uncertainty from membership matrix — compute_uncertainty
text: function computes entropy-based uncertainty observation using membership vector identifies uncertain locations based either mean-plus-SD threshold quantile threshold.

title: Compute uncertainty from membership matrix — compute_uncertainty
code: compute_uncertainty(   u,   normalized = TRUE,   threshold_method = c("mean_sd", "quantile"),   threshold_scale = 0.5,   quantile_prob = NULL )

title: Compute uncertainty from membership matrix — compute_uncertainty
text: u numeric matrix dimension \(n \times k\), \(n\) number observations \(k\) number clusters. row represent membership vector. normalized logical value indicating whether return normalized entropy. threshold_method character string specifying thresholding method. Must one "mean_sd" "quantile". threshold_scale numeric value specifying multiplier standard deviation threshold_method = "mean_sd". quantile_prob numeric value 0 1 specifying quantile probability threshold_method = "quantile". Ignored otherwise.

title: Compute uncertainty from membership matrix — compute_uncertainty
text: list containing: entropy: numeric vector entropy values. threshold: threshold entropy used define uncertainty. loc_uncertain: Indices uncertain observations. is_uncertain: logical vector indicating uncertain observations. threshold_method: thresholding method used. threshold_scale: SD multiplier used threshold_method = "mean_sd". quantile_prob: quantile probability used threshold_method = "quantile", NULL otherwise. normalized: Whether entropy normalized.

title: Calculate the XB index — compute_xb
text: Compute Xie-Beni cluster validity index data, membership matrix, cluster centers.

title: Calculate the XB index — compute_xb
code: compute_xb(x, u, v, m = 2)

title: Calculate the XB index — compute_xb
text: x numeric matrix dimension \(n \times d\), \(n\) number observations \(d\) number features. u numeric matrix dimension \(n \times k\), \(n\) number observations \(k\) number clusters. row represent membership vector. v numeric matrix dimension \(k \times d\), \(k\) number clusters \(d\) number features. row represent cluster center. m numeric value greater 1 specifying fuzziness parameter.

title: Calculate the XB index — compute_xb
text: numeric value representing XB index.

title: Detect aggregated uncertain locations — detect_aggregation
text: Identify aggregated locations among uncertain observations using three-step procedure: Step 1: Select uncertain locations least specified number (proportion) uncertain neighbors among \(n\_neighbors\) nearest neighbors. Step 2: Expand aggregation set adding uncertain neighbors Step 1 aggregation locations. Step 3: Build igraph connectivity graph Step 2 aggregation locations remove small connected components.

title: Detect aggregated uncertain locations — detect_aggregation
code: detect_aggregation(   dat_loc,   is_uncertain,   n_neighbors,   threshold_count = NULL,   threshold_prop = NULL,   add_high_uncertain_step1 = FALSE,   high_uncertain = NULL,   do_step2 = TRUE,   distance_threshold = NULL,   min_component_size = NULL )

title: Detect aggregated uncertain locations — detect_aggregation
text: dat_loc numeric matrix dimension \(n \times \rho\), \(n\) number observations \(\rho\) spatial coordinate dimension (example, \(\rho = 2\) 2D coordinates). row corresponds one observation. is_uncertain logical vector length \(n\) indicating whether observation uncertain. n_neighbors integer specifying number nearest neighbors. threshold_count integer specifying minimum number uncertain neighbors required Step 1. provided, takes precedence threshold_prop. threshold_prop numeric value 0 1 specifying minimum proportion uncertain neighbors required Step 1. Used threshold_count = NULL. add_high_uncertain_step1 logical value indicating whether forcibly add highly uncertain locations Step 1. high_uncertain Either logical vector length \(n\) integer vector indices specifying highly uncertain locations added Step 1 add_high_uncertain_step1 = TRUE. do_step2 logical value indicating whether expand aggregation locations adding uncertain neighbors Step 1 aggregation locations. distance_threshold positive numeric value specifying distance threshold used define graph connectivity Step 3. NULL, automatically set 1.1 times median nearest-neighbor distance. min_component_size integer specifying minimum connected component size keep Step 3. NULL, automatically set floor(0.003 * nrow(dat_loc)).

title: Detect aggregated uncertain locations — detect_aggregation
text: list containing: loc_aggre: Indices final aggregation locations. is_aggre: logical vector length \(n\) indicating uncertain aggregation status. aggre_cluster: integer vector length \(n\) giving reordered igraph component labels final aggregation locations, NA otherwise. loc_uncertain: Indices uncertain locations. loc_certain: Indices certain locations. n_aggre: Number final aggregation locations. prop_aggre_in_uncertain: Proportion final aggregation locations among uncertain locations.

title: Detect aggregated uncertain locations — detect_aggregation
text: Optionally, highly uncertain locations (example, locations entropy greater mean + 1.5 sd) can forcibly added Step 1.

title: Detect boundary uncertain locations — detect_boundary
text: Identify boundary locations among uncertain observations based spatial nearest neighbors. uncertain location defined boundary location : least one certain location among \(n_neighbors\) nearest neighbors; \(n_neighbors\) nearest neighbors contain least two distinct cluster labels; cluster label represented among \(n_neighbors\) nearest neighbors.

title: Detect boundary uncertain locations — detect_boundary
code: detect_boundary(dat_loc, cluster, is_uncertain, n_neighbors)

title: Detect boundary uncertain locations — detect_boundary
text: dat_loc numeric matrix dimension \(n \times d\), \(n\) number observations \(d\) number spatial coordinates. row corresponds one observation. cluster vector length \(n\) giving hard cluster labels. is_uncertain logical vector length \(n\) indicating whether observation uncertain. n_neighbors integer specifying number nearest neighbors.

title: Detect boundary uncertain locations — detect_boundary
text: list containing: loc_boundary: Indices boundary uncertain locations. is_boundary: logical vector length \(n\) indicating uncertain boundary status. loc_uncertain: Indices uncertain locations. loc_certain: Indices certain locations. n_boundary: Number boundary uncertain locations. prop_boundary_in_uncertain: Proportion boundary uncertain locations among uncertain locations.

title: Fuzzy c-means clustering — fcmclust
text: Perform fuzzy c-means clustering multiple random initializations return best solution based final objective value.

title: Fuzzy c-means clustering — fcmclust
code: fcmclust(   x,   nclus,   m = 2,   nstart = 10,   iter_max = 100,   reltol = 1e-04,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Fuzzy c-means clustering — fcmclust
text: x numeric matrix dimension \(n \times d\), \(n\) number observations \(d\) number features. nclus integer specifying number clusters. m numeric value greater 1 specifying fuzziness parameter. nstart integer specifying number random initializations. iter_max integer specifying maximum number iterations initialization. reltol numeric value specifying relative tolerance convergence. verbose logical value indicating whether print convergence messages.

title: Fuzzy c-means clustering — fcmclust
text: object class "fcmclust" containing: nclus: Number clusters. m: Fuzziness parameter. nstart: Number random initializations. membership: Fuzzy membership matrix. cluster: Hard cluster labels. size: Cluster sizes. centers: Cluster centers. withinerror: Final objective value. iter: Number iterations convergence. call: Matched function call.

what: Fuzzy c-means clustering with automatic fuzziness parameter selection — fcmclust_auto
title: Fuzzy c-means clustering with automatic fuzziness parameter selection — fcmclust_auto
text: Run fuzzy c-means clustering grid candidate fuzziness parameters, select best fuzziness parameter using XB index, return corresponding clustering result.

title: Fuzzy c-means clustering with automatic fuzziness parameter selection — fcmclust_auto
code: fcmclust_auto(   x,   nclus,   vec_m = seq(from = 1.1, to = 2.5, by = 0.1),   nstart = 10,   iter_max = 100,   reltol = 1e-04,   verbose = FALSE,   return_all_fits = FALSE )

title: Fuzzy c-means clustering with automatic fuzziness parameter selection — fcmclust_auto
text: x numeric matrix dimension \(n \times d\), \(n\) number observations \(d\) number features. nclus integer specifying number clusters. vec_m numeric vector specifying candidate fuzziness parameters. nstart integer specifying number random initializations. iter_max integer specifying maximum number iterations. reltol numeric value specifying relative tolerance convergence. verbose logical value indicating whether print convergence messages. return_all_fits logical value indicating whether return fitted models vec_m.

title: Fuzzy c-means clustering with automatic fuzziness parameter selection — fcmclust_auto
text: object class "fcmclust_auto" (inheriting "fcmclust"), corresponding selected fuzziness parameter, additional components: best_m: selected fuzziness parameter. xb: XB index selected solution. vec_m: Candidate fuzziness parameters. xb_grid: XB index values corresponding vec_m. all_fits: list fitted models candidate values m return_all_fits = TRUE, otherwise NULL.

title: Fuzzy c-means clustering with automatic fuzziness parameter selection — fcmclust_auto
text: recommended default interface users.

text: Visualize aggregated uncertain locations

title: Visualize aggregated uncertain locations — plot_aggregation
